title: R07: Preserve exact spec literals during planner bead generation
description: Fix planner bead creation so inline code spans, fenced code blocks, file paths, commands, quotes, angle brackets, and shell metacharacters are preserved instead of being dropped by quoting or parsing.
acceptance_criteria: Generated beads retain command and path literals from specs; acceptance criteria do not silently drop backticked content; tests cover inline code, fenced code, quotes, angle brackets, and shell metacharacters.

title: R06: Make planner runs idempotent and recoverable after timeouts
description: Assign stable planning run metadata, record created beads, detect prior work for a spec/run, and summarize partial, complete, failed, or unknown planning states after timeout.
acceptance_criteria: Retrying planner-plan after timeout does not duplicate beads; Purser can list beads created for a spec/run; planner-created beads include spec and planner-run metadata.
notes: Context from beads-21r.1: planner-plan currently captures before/after IDs only around a successful Pi role process in src/purser/planner.py. Timeout in src/purser/roles.py raises before after-state capture/artifact writing. Add stable spec/run identity, metadata on created beads, retry duplicate detection, and partial/unknown summaries. See docs/plans/2026-04-27-purser-reliability-audit.md.

title: R04: Align executor and reviewer prompts with orchestrator lifecycle
description: Update static role prompts and runtime appended instructions so they agree with the lifecycle model selected in R01.
acceptance_criteria: No prompt instructs an agent to both move a bead to review and leave it for Purser; lifecycle transitions are documented; failed runs leave clear status/recovery evidence.
notes: Context from beads-21r.1: lifecycle decision is Purser-owned transitions. Reviewer prompts in src/purser/prompts/reviewer.md and runtime reviewer message in src/purser/loop.py currently require the reviewer to close/reopen beads; remove that requirement. Executor prompts are mostly aligned but should continue to forbid self-close/custom statuses. See docs/plans/2026-04-27-purser-reliability-audit.md.

title: R03: Implement bounded structured-output repair retries
description: When a completed agent response lacks a valid outcome, retry with the transcript and schema requesting only corrected structured output, then fail clearly if still invalid.
acceptance_criteria: Missing outcomes, malformed JSON, and schema validation failures trigger bounded repair attempts; repair attempts are stored in run records; Purser does not fabricate success when evidence is insufficient.
notes: Implemented bounded structured-output repair in src/purser/loop.py. Executor/reviewer parse failures now trigger one repair role call with raw final text, JSON-mode transcript, validation error, and schema contract; repair output must be JSON only and is parsed through the same validators. Run artifacts record repair_attempts for success and failure paths. Tests cover missing executor outcome repair, malformed reviewer outcome repair, and failed repair attempts. Validation: uv run pytest; uv run ruff check src tests; uv run pyright.
close_reason: Bounded executor/reviewer structured outcome repair implemented with artifact recording and tests.

title: R02: Define schema-native executor and reviewer outcome contracts
description: Add explicit executor and reviewer outcome schemas, validators, typed representations, and run-record fields for raw output plus parsed structured outcome.
acceptance_criteria: Executor and reviewer outcomes validate required fields and enums with clear errors; run records persist raw output and parsed outcomes; existing successful flows remain compatible or have a migration path.
notes:
Context from beads-21r.1: current outcome parsing is in src/purser/outcomes.py and depends on Markdown fenced JSON in final_text extracted by src/purser/roles.py. Artifacts already preserve raw role output and parsed outcomes when reached. Implement schema-native executor/reviewer outcomes with required-field and enum validation; reviewer should move from decision/state_transition_performed to status values approved|rejected|blocked|failed; executor should include gates_run and blocking_reason. See docs/plans/2026-04-27-purser-reliability-audit.md.
Implemented schema-native outcome primitives in src/purser/outcomes.py: explicit executor/reviewer schema constants, GateOutcome and ReviewIssue dataclasses, enum validation for executor/reviewer/gates/issues, native dict/raw JSON parsing, and fenced JSON compatibility. Run artifacts now preserve reviewer compatibility aliases alongside the parsed structured outcome. Validation run: uv run pytest; uv run ruff check src tests; uv run pyright.
close_reason: Implemented schema-native executor/reviewer outcome contracts and validation with compatibility path; tests and checks pass.

title: R01: Audit structured-output surfaces and decide lifecycle ownership
description: Audit current planner, executor, reviewer, prompt, run-record, and Beads integration paths affected by the reliability spec. Record the authoritative lifecycle model for which component changes Beads state.
design: Audit recorded in docs/plans/2026-04-27-purser-reliability-audit.md. Lifecycle decision: Purser orchestration owns executor/reviewer Beads state transitions. Agents emit structured outcomes and may record notes/follow-up beads, but normal status/review-ready/close/reopen transitions are performed by Purser after validating outcomes and gates. Custom in_review remains compatibility-only; purser_review_ready metadata is the portable queue signal.
acceptance_criteria: Current flow map is documented in the bead or linked notes; lifecycle owner is explicitly decided; prompt contradictions are identified; implementation subtasks have enough context to proceed.
notes: Flow map and implementation context documented in docs/plans/2026-04-27-purser-reliability-audit.md. Key prompt contradiction: reviewer prompts currently require the reviewer agent to close/reopen beads; this conflicts with the selected Purser-owned lifecycle model and must be corrected in beads-21r.4. Executor prompts are already broadly aligned with Purser-owned review readiness; parser/output transport remains the weak point.
close_reason: Audit complete: flow map, lifecycle decision, prompt contradictions, and downstream implementation context documented in docs/plans/2026-04-27-purser-reliability-audit.md.

title: Epic: Purser execution reliability and structured outcomes
description: Implement the Purser reliability spec from consumer-agent diagnostics. The work makes planner/executor/reviewer flows schema-native, recoverable after partial output or timeouts, and easier to diagnose without manual run-record archaeology.
acceptance_criteria: All acceptance criteria in specs/2026-04-27-purser-execution-reliability-and-structured-outcomes.md are satisfied; automated tests cover structured outcomes, repair retries, decision beads, planner idempotency, literal preservation, warnings, and failure summaries; documentation explains contracts, lifecycle ownership, and recovery commands.

title: B05: Expand purser init into full Pi-native scaffold generation
description: Expand `purser init` into the main Pi-native portable adoption entry point. Scaffold `.purser.toml`, prompt files, `specs/`, local Purser guidance, and use repo-root resolution plus safe merge/update helpers.
acceptance_criteria:
- `purser init` creates a full Pi-native scaffold in repo root
- repeated init runs are idempotent
- `purser init --force` behavior is explicit and safe
- tests cover nested-directory setup and re-run behavior
notes: Expanded purser init into a fuller Pi-native scaffold step. It now writes the new prompt taxonomy layout under .purser/prompts/roles and .purser/prompts/workflows, creates specs/.gitkeep, creates .purser/README.md, uses repo-root resolution, and is idempotent by default while supporting --force overwrites. Updated src/purser/resources.py, src/purser/cli.py, and tests/test_cli.py. Verified with: uv run pytest -q (47 passed).
close_reason: Review pass: purser init now generates the agreed Pi-native scaffold layout, creates the expected local files, and is idempotent by default with explicit --force behavior.

title: B04: Build safe merge/update helpers for scaffold-managed files
description: Build reusable, tested merge/update helpers for repo-managed markdown, JSON, and ignore files so Purser can safely maintain AGENTS.md, .pi/settings.json, and .gitignore.
acceptance_criteria:
- AGENTS.md helper preserves unrelated content and updates a delimited Purser-owned section idempotently
- .pi/settings.json helper merges prompts entries without removing unrelated keys
- .gitignore helper appends missing entries without duplication
- tests cover idempotency and preservation behavior
notes: Added reusable scaffold helpers in src/purser/scaffold.py for delimited markdown section upsert, .pi/settings.json prompt merging, and additive .gitignore updates. Added focused tests in tests/test_scaffold.py covering creation, preservation, replacement, and idempotency. Verified with: uv run pytest -q (46 passed).
close_reason: Review pass: scaffold helpers are reusable, narrowly scoped, preserve unrelated content, and are directly covered by idempotency/preservation tests.

title: B03: Add repo-root resolution and adopt it in CLI paths
description: Add repo-root detection utilities and adopt them in CLI entry points so Purser behaves sensibly from nested directories inside a repository.
acceptance_criteria:
- core commands run from a nested directory resolve the repo root correctly
- tests cover repo-root resolution behavior
- root behavior remains correct when already invoked from repo root
notes: Added repo-root resolution in src/purser/repo.py with Git-aware lookup plus fallbacks for .purser.toml, .beads, and .git. Wired cli init, runtime config loading, and doctor through resolve_repo_root(). Added nested-directory tests in tests/test_cli.py for init and doctor. Verified with: uv run pytest -q (40 passed).
close_reason: Review pass: repo-root resolution is now applied to init, runtime config loading, and doctor, with focused nested-directory test coverage and acceptable fallback behavior.

title: B02: Define Pi-native prompt taxonomy and naming contract
description: Define the Pi-native prompt taxonomy and public naming contract before scaffold generation changes land. Cover runtime role prompts, operator workflow prompts, on-disk layout under `.purser/`, and documented Pi slash-command mapping.
acceptance_criteria:
- one documented prompt taxonomy covers role prompts vs workflow prompts
- prompt filenames and command names are explicit and stable
- naming decisions are reflected in docs/architecture inputs used by later beads
notes: Defined the Pi-native prompt taxonomy and naming contract in docs/architecture/pi-native-prompt-taxonomy.md. Chose separate runtime role prompts and operator workflow prompts, with `.pi/settings.json` intended to point at `.purser/prompts/workflows`. Added a README pointer to the new design note. Verified with: uv run pytest -q (38 passed).
close_reason: Review pass: prompt taxonomy and naming contract are explicit, Pi-native, and sufficiently concrete for later scaffold work without overreaching into implementation.

title: B01: Align failing tests and planner approval wording
description: Fix the immediate drift between implementation and tests so the repository has a trustworthy green baseline before broader refactoring. Align planner approval wording between current implementation and tests with minimal behavioral change.
acceptance_criteria:
- `uv run pytest -q` passes
- planner approval wording is consistent between implementation and tests
- no unrelated behavior changes are introduced
notes: Aligned tests with the current planner approval contract by updating tests/test_planner.py to expect approval-before-planning wording. Verified with: uv run pytest -q (38 passed).
close_reason: Review pass: test expectation now matches the current planner approval-before-planning contract, the change is minimal, and uv run pytest -q is green.

title: Epic: Refactor Purser into a Pi-native portable framework
description:
Refactor Purser into a Pi-native, portable orchestration framework per:
- /home/md/src/repos/beads/specs/2026-04-23-pi-native-portable-purser-refactor.md
- /home/md/src/repos/beads/docs/plans/2026-04-23-pi-native-portable-purser-refactor-plan.md

This epic tracks the reviewed bead graph for the adoption-first refactor: baseline alignment, Pi-native scaffold/portability, runtime protocol hardening, and final validation.
acceptance_criteria:
- The approved refactor work is represented as dependent child beads covering baseline alignment, portability, runtime hardening, and final validation
- Child beads preserve the reviewed sequencing and critical dependencies from the planning artifact
- The epic remains open until the refactor outcome satisfies the approved spec
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Epic: Refactor Purser into a Pi-native portable framework
description:
Refactor Purser into a Pi-native, portable orchestration framework per:
- /home/md/src/repos/beads/specs/2026-04-23-pi-native-portable-purser-refactor.md
- /home/md/src/repos/beads/docs/plans/2026-04-23-pi-native-portable-purser-refactor-plan.md

This epic tracks the reviewed bead graph for the adoption-first refactor: baseline alignment, Pi-native scaffold/portability, runtime protocol hardening, and docs/polish.
acceptance_criteria:
- The approved refactor work is represented as dependent child beads covering baseline alignment, portability, runtime hardening, and final validation
- Child beads preserve the reviewed sequencing and critical dependencies from the planning artifact
- The epic remains open until the refactor outcome satisfies the approved spec
close_reason: Duplicate epic created during aborted graph creation; superseded by beads-7pe

title: R09: Add reliability regression tests and workflow documentation
description: Add automated tests and docs covering the end-to-end reliability cases from the spec, using fake or stubbed model responses where possible.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover valid outcomes, repair, malformed JSON, validation diagnostics, decision beads, prompt consistency, planner retry, literal preservation, warnings, and failure summaries; docs describe structured outcome contract, lifecycle ownership, and recovery flow.

title: R08: Capture Beads warnings and emit actionable recovery diagnostics
description: Capture Beads warnings such as auto-export git add failures, include them in run records and summaries, and make executor/reviewer failures print run path, bead status, git status, and suggested safe next commands.
acceptance_criteria: Run summaries include Beads warnings and reproduction context; structured outcome failures include run record path, bead status, git status summary, and recovery commands; documentation explains repo-local embedded Beads expectations.
notes: Context from beads-21r.1: BeadsClient._run in src/purser/beads.py discards stderr on successful bd commands, hiding warnings such as auto-export git add failures. Capture warnings into artifacts/summaries and include failure diagnostics with artifact path, bead status, git status summary, and recovery commands. See docs/plans/2026-04-27-purser-reliability-audit.md.

title: R05: Add first-class decision bead execution path
description: Route decision-type beads through a decision-specific executor path that can complete without code changes or gates while recording decisions and follow-up beads.
acceptance_criteria: Decision beads can succeed with files_touched=[] and gates_run=[]; decision comments are visible via bd show; follow-up beads for deferrals are captured in the structured outcome.

title: Make Purser review handoff independent of custom Beads statuses
description: Update Purser so exec-build does not require bd to accept custom in_review status. Use built-in Beads statuses plus Purser-owned metadata for review readiness, while preserving compatibility with existing in_review beads where supported.
acceptance_criteria:
- executor completion can mark a bead ready for review without setting status to in_review
- review queue discovers metadata-marked review-ready beads and existing in_review beads when supported
- tests cover environments where bd rejects in_review
- docs/prompts no longer require custom in_review status
close_reason: Implemented portable Purser review handoff: executor now leaves work in built-in statuses and marks purser_review_ready metadata, review queue discovers metadata-ready beads while optionally supporting legacy in_review, prompts/config avoid custom review statuses, package version bumped to 0.1.1, and tests cover bd status rejection fallback.

title: Remove stray sample hello files from repo
description: HELLO.md and HELLO2.md were added while closing leftover sample/demo beads. They should not be part of this repository and must be removed from the working tree and GitHub remote history tip.
close_reason: Removed stray HELLO sample files from repo and Git tip

title: B20: Add end-to-end smoke coverage for setup and hardened runtime
description: Add final end-to-end smoke coverage for portable setup and hardened runtime, including nested-directory setup, structured reviewer decisions, and representative integration paths.
acceptance_criteria:
- representative end-to-end or integration-style tests cover the critical path
- the refactor is validated beyond isolated unit tests
- the final test suite passes
notes: Added final integration-style smoke coverage in tests/test_smoke.py. The new smoke tests cover (1) portable setup from a nested repo directory through real `purser init` scaffolding plus successful `purser doctor` readiness output, and (2) hardened runtime behavior through a full executor/reviewer loop with structured outcomes, validation logging, and persisted runtime artifacts under `.purser/runs/`. This gives representative end-to-end coverage across nested-directory setup, Pi prompt integration/readiness messaging, structured reviewer decisions, and artifact persistence. Verified with: uv run pytest -q (74 passed).
close_reason: Review pass and explicitly approved for closure despite open B19: final smoke coverage exercises the critical portable-setup and hardened-runtime paths, and the project decision is that there are no legacy Purser adopters requiring migration/compatibility handling before this validation bead closes.

title: B19: Add migration and compatibility handling for layout/config changes
description: Add migration/compatibility handling for changed prompt layout or config expectations. Auto-migrate safe cases, preserve user-edited files, and fail clearly when automatic migration is unsafe.
acceptance_criteria:
- users with older layout receive actionable migration guidance or safe compatibility behavior
- compatibility behavior is documented
- tests cover at least one legacy-to-new-layout detection path
close_reason: Closed

title: B18: Rewrite README and adoption docs around Pi-native Option A
description: Rewrite README and adoption docs around Option A so they match actual CLI behavior, generated files, Pi-native prompt usage, and the intended model posture.
acceptance_criteria:
- README and setup docs reflect Option A clearly
- docs match generated files and CLI behavior
- docs explain Codex-through-Pi and open-model-friendly examples correctly
notes: Rewrote the top-level README, consumer setup guide, and adoption-agent template around the implemented Pi-native Option A architecture. Docs now match the actual scaffold/layout from `purser init` (`.purser/prompts/roles/*`, `.purser/prompts/workflows/*`, `.pi/settings.json` -> `../.purser/prompts/workflows`, `AGENTS.md` markers, `.gitignore` additions), the explicit `approve-plan` step before `planner-plan`, and the current model posture (`roles.models.<role>` -> `roles.default_model` -> Pi ambient/default fallback, with examples like qwen3.5/gemma4/gpt-oss/Codex-through-Pi). Also updated doctor/setup wording to match the strengthened Pi-native readiness checks and documented `.purser/runs/` runtime artifacts. Verified with: uv run pytest -q (72 passed).
close_reason: Review pass: README and adoption docs now match the implemented Pi-native Option A behavior, scaffold layout, approval flow, prompt discovery, model posture, doctor output, and runtime artifacts.

title: B17: Strengthen purser doctor for Pi-native readiness
description: Strengthen `purser doctor` for Pi-native readiness, including prompt integration, prompt layout consistency, model configuration/fallback messaging, and existing binary/config/beads checks.
acceptance_criteria:
- purser doctor reports Pi-native readiness more completely
- missing prompt integration is surfaced clearly
- model configuration versus Pi-default fallback is described honestly
- tests cover new doctor output cases
notes: Strengthened `purser doctor` for Pi-native readiness. Added explicit doctor sections for binaries/config/prompts/models/beads, plus runtime health checks for `.pi/settings.json` workflow prompt integration, prompt-layout consistency under `.purser/prompts/{roles,workflows}`, and model configuration hygiene. Doctor now reports sensible ambient/default Pi fallback when no repo models are pinned, and warns clearly when configured model strings are blank or prompt integration is missing. Added focused CLI tests for missing Pi prompt integration, blank model strings, and Pi-default fallback messaging while preserving existing repo-root and Beads-storage checks. Verified with: uv run pytest -q (72 passed).
close_reason: Review pass: purser doctor now reports materially stronger Pi-native readiness, including prompt integration/layout and honest model fallback messaging, with focused CLI coverage.

title: B16: Add runtime artifact persistence for transcripts, outcomes, and gate evidence
description: Add runtime artifact persistence for transcripts, structured outcomes, and gate evidence under a stable local location such as `.purser/runs/`, with moderate evidence by default.
acceptance_criteria:
- runtime artifacts are written to a predictable local location
- artifacts include enough information to debug planner/executor/reviewer failures
- tests cover artifact creation paths where practical
notes: Added runtime artifact persistence under .purser/runs/ via src/purser/artifacts.py. Planner, executor, and reviewer flows now write compact JSON artifacts capturing role transcripts/final text, structured outcomes when parseable, gate evidence, key status context, and explicit errors on malformed payloads or gate failures. Planner artifacts include before/after/created bead IDs; reviewer approval artifacts also reference the validation log path. Added practical tests covering happy-path and malformed-output artifact creation for planner and executor flows. Verified with: uv run pytest -q (69 passed).
close_reason: Review pass: runtime artifacts are now persisted under .purser/runs with useful planner/executor/reviewer evidence, including transcripts, structured outcomes, gate evidence, and error context, with practical test coverage.

title: B15: Migrate reviewer flow away from regex-first approval parsing
description: Migrate reviewer flow away from regex-first approval parsing to structured reviewer decisions anchored to actual Beads state transitions while preserving validation logging.
acceptance_criteria:
- reviewer decisions are processed primarily through structured data
- approval/rejection no longer depends primarily on prose regexes
- validation log is still written correctly for approved beads
- tests cover approve, reject, malformed output, and missing transition cases
notes: Migrated reviewer flow to structured outcomes as the primary contract. PurserLoop._review() now requires a fenced JSON reviewer payload with decision, bead_id, state_transition_performed, issues, and summary; it fails clearly when that payload is missing or malformed, when bead_id mismatches, or when the Beads state transition does not match the reviewer decision. The old regex-first approval parsing path was removed. Rejection handling now always records the reviewer summary back into Beads as an open-note. Updated reviewer-related end-to-end loop tests to use the new structured contract and added explicit failure coverage for prose-only reviewer output. Verified with: uv run pytest -q (69 passed).
close_reason: Review pass: reviewer flow now uses structured reviewer outcomes instead of regex-first prose parsing, enforces real Beads state transitions, preserves validation logging, and is covered by rejection/malformed-output tests.

title: B14: Migrate executor flow to structured outcomes
description: Migrate executor flow to structured outcomes so Purser can validate completion claims, files touched, follow-up bead creation, and review readiness without allowing self-close.
acceptance_criteria:
- executor emits parseable structured outcomes
- executor self-close prevention remains enforced
- tests cover structured executor outcomes and state validation
notes: Migrated executor flow to require structured outcomes. PurserLoop._execute() now instructs the executor to emit a fenced JSON payload with status, bead_id, files_touched, new_beads, ready_for_review, and summary; it fails clearly when that payload is missing or malformed, when bead_id mismatches, or when ready_for_review is false. Updated executor-focused unit and end-to-end loop tests to use the new structured contract and added explicit failure coverage for missing payloads. Verified with: uv run pytest -q (69 passed).
close_reason: Review pass: executor flow now requires structured outcomes, preserves executor self-close prevention, validates bead identity/review readiness, and is covered by loop tests.

title: B13: Migrate planner flow to structured outcomes and approval enforcement
description: Migrate planner flow to structured outcomes and approval enforcement. Validate created bead IDs and required planning effects against Beads state.
acceptance_criteria:
- planner emits parseable structured outcomes
- Purser validates created bead IDs and required planning effects
- planner fails clearly when approval or planning contract requirements are unmet
- tests cover successful and unsuccessful planner outcomes
notes: Migrated planner flow to require structured outcomes plus actual Beads mutations. PlannerService.plan_spec() now parses a fenced JSON planner payload, fails clearly when the payload is missing or malformed, and validates that outcome.created_beads matches the actual bead IDs created during the run. The planner prompt contract now explicitly requires a structured outcome with status, created_beads, dependencies, needs_human_input, and summary. Added planner tests covering valid structured outcomes, missing payloads, and mismatched created bead IDs. Verified with: uv run pytest -q (68 passed).
close_reason: Review pass: planner flow now enforces explicit approval, requires structured planner outcomes, validates created bead IDs against actual Beads mutations, and fails clearly on contract violations.

title: B12: Add explicit planning approval mechanism
description: Add an explicit planning approval mechanism using a CLI approval command backed by repo-local state, integrated with `human_approve_plan = true`.
acceptance_criteria:
- planning approval can be enforced mechanically
- clear user-facing errors or instructions appear when approval is missing
- tests cover approved and unapproved planning flows
notes: Added explicit planning approval backed by repo-local state in src/purser/approvals.py and enforced it in PlannerService.plan_spec() when human_approve_plan is enabled. Added a new CLI command `purser approve-plan <spec>` that records approval under .purser/state/plan-approvals/. Planner now fails clearly with an approval-required message when approval is missing. Added tests in tests/test_planner.py and tests/test_cli.py for approval enforcement and approval-state creation. Verified with: uv run pytest -q (66 passed).
close_reason: Review pass: explicit plan approval is implemented via purser approve-plan and repo-local approval state under .purser/state/plan-approvals, with planner enforcement and CLI/test coverage.

title: B11: Introduce structured role outcome parsing primitives
description: Introduce structured role outcome parsing primitives for planner, executor, and reviewer so Purser can validate machine-readable role results instead of relying on prose inference.
acceptance_criteria:
- parser helpers extract and validate structured planner/executor/reviewer payloads from Pi output
- invalid or missing payloads fail clearly
- tests cover valid and invalid role payload parsing
notes: Added structured role outcome parsing primitives in src/purser/outcomes.py. Implemented typed parser functions for planner, executor, and reviewer outcomes, using fenced JSON payload extraction plus explicit field validation and clear OutcomeProtocolError messages. Added focused tests in tests/test_outcomes.py for valid planner/executor/reviewer payloads, missing payloads, invalid JSON, and wrong field types. Verified with: uv run pytest -q (64 passed).
close_reason: Review pass: structured planner/executor/reviewer outcome parsers now exist, validate fenced JSON payloads with clear protocol errors, and are covered by focused parser tests.

title: B10: Update config and docs for Pi-native model posture and fallback
description: Update config defaults and docs for Pi-native open-model posture. Support optional per-role model overrides, optional shared default_model, and fallback to Pi ambient/default model when no explicit override is configured.
acceptance_criteria:
- starter config does not scaffold working-looking per-role model defaults
- Purser supports optional `roles.default_model` and optional per-role overrides
- docs describe Codex-through-Pi and Pi-routed gpt-oss, gemma4, qwen3.5, and Ollama Cloud examples without treating them as defaults
- implementation/docs validate the intended model resolution order clearly
notes: Updated Purser model posture to avoid scaffolded working-looking defaults while preserving optional repo pinning. Config now supports roles.default_model plus optional per-role overrides, and roles.resolved_model() falls back to Pi ambient/default selection when nothing is configured. PiRunner now omits --model when model is unresolved, enabling Pi-default behavior. The starter config now documents model options as commented examples rather than live defaults. Added tests in tests/test_config.py, tests/test_roles.py, and tests/test_cli.py. Verified with: uv run pytest -q (58 passed).
close_reason: Review pass: model posture now avoids scaffolded live defaults, supports optional repo-level and per-role overrides, and correctly falls back to Pi ambient/default model selection by omitting --model when nothing is pinned.

title: B09: Add repo-type detection and strict starter gate inference
description: Add repo-type detection and strict starter gate inference when repo signals are strong, especially for modern Python repos using uv/ruff/ty.
acceptance_criteria:
- Python/uv repos receive uv-based starter gates when evidence is strong
- other supported repo types receive reasonable starter inference or clear fallback behavior
- weak-signal repos do not get misleading invented commands
- tests cover repo-type detection and gate selection
notes: Added repo-type/init-profile detection in src/purser/detect.py and wired purser init to use it when generating starter gates. Strong Python+uv repos now get uv-based gates, preferring ty when present and otherwise pyright. Node repos use available package.json scripts when present, and unknown repos fall back safely to empty gate commands. Added focused tests in tests/test_detect.py plus CLI coverage in tests/test_cli.py. Verified with: uv run pytest -q (56 passed).
close_reason: Review pass: repo-type detection now provides strict uv-based starter gates when Python repo signals are strong, conservative fallback behavior otherwise, and focused test coverage across supported cases.

title: B08: Implement .gitignore runtime-artifact integration
description: Implement `.gitignore` runtime-artifact integration so local Purser and Beads files can be ignored safely and additively during adoption.
acceptance_criteria:
- .gitignore receives Purser-related ignore entries only when missing
- existing .gitignore content is preserved
- tests cover additive behavior and idempotency
notes: Integrated additive .gitignore updates into purser init using the scaffold helper. Init now appends the recommended local runtime entries (.beads/, .purser/, .purser.toml, VALIDATION.md) only when missing, while preserving existing ignore content. Added CLI tests covering default creation and additive append behavior. Verified with: uv run pytest -q (50 passed).
close_reason: Review pass: .gitignore integration is additive, avoids duplicates, preserves existing content, and adds only the intended local runtime entries.

title: B07: Implement AGENTS.md Purser-owned section management
description: Implement AGENTS.md Purser-owned section management with Pi-native workflow guidance and the human approval boundary while preserving unrelated instructions.
acceptance_criteria:
- AGENTS.md is created if missing or updated if present
- the Purser section states Purser is tooling/framework, not the repo product unless explicitly requested
- repeated init does not duplicate the section
- tests cover create, update, replace, and preserve cases
notes: Integrated AGENTS.md Purser-owned section management into purser init using the delimited markdown helper. Init now creates or updates AGENTS.md with a single Purser workflow section while preserving unrelated content. Added CLI tests covering default creation and replacement/preservation of an existing Purser-owned section. Verified with: uv run pytest -q (49 passed).
close_reason: Review pass: AGENTS.md integration maintains a single replaceable Purser-owned section, preserves unrelated content, and captures the intended Pi-native workflow guidance.

title: B06: Implement .pi/settings.json prompt-directory integration
description: Implement `.pi/settings.json` prompt-directory integration so operator workflow prompts are discoverable by Pi after `/reload`.
acceptance_criteria:
- `.pi/settings.json` contains the expected prompt directory entry after init
